description: Chinese transnational organized crime syndicates based in Greater China with global outposts. Originated from 18th-century secret societies aimed at overthrowing the Qing dynasty. After the PRC's founding, triads retreated to Hong Kong, Macau, and overseas Chinese communities. Modern triads are involved in diverse criminal activities including drug trafficking, human trafficking, and money laundering.

country_links.evidence_quote: Triads are also active in other regions with significant overseas-Chinese populations: Hong Kong, Macau, Taiwan, South Korea, Japan, the United States, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, the United Kingdom, France, Italy, the Netherlands, Belgium, Germany, Spain, Brazil, Peru, Argentina, Romania, Bulgaria, Russia, and South Africa.
country_links.context: Triads are active in Argentina.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: Mainland China , Hong Kong , Macau , Southeast Asia , Japan , South Korea , Taiwan , North America , Brazil , Argentina , Peru , Australia , New Zealand , Canada , United Kingdom , France , Italy , Netherlands , Belgium , Germany , Spain , Romania , Bulgaria , Russia , and South Africa
country_links.context: The Triad has territory in Australia.

country_links.evidence_quote: most traditional Chinese secret societies, including the triads and some of the remaining Green Gang, relocated to Hong Kong, Taiwan , Southeast Asia , and overseas countries (particularly the United States )
country_links.context: Triads relocated to the United States and compete with other ethnic Chinese criminal organizations there.
